recipes.name: Zelena mana
recipes.description: Traditional Dalmatian green bean soup from Dalmatia. Simple, nourishing, and perfect for a family meal.
instructions: Trim and string green beans. Cut into 3cm pieces.
Peel and cube potatoes. Dice carrots and celery.
Sauté onion in olive oil until soft. Add garlic, cook 1 min.
Stir in tomato paste, then add potatoes and carrots.
Pour in water, bring to boil. Add beans. Simmer 20 min.
Season with salt and pepper. Cook until beans are tender.
Finish with fresh parsley. Serve with crusty bread.

recipes.name: Zagorska grašica
recipes.description: Traditional Zagorje bean soup. Warming, filling, and authentically Croatian vegetarian comfort food.
instructions: Soak beans overnight. Drain and rinse.
Sauté onion, carrots, celery in olive oil until soft.
Add garlic and paprika, cook 2 min. Stir in flour.
Add passata and broth, bring to boil. Add beans and bay leaves.
Simmer 1.5 hours until beans are creamy and tender.
Mash some beans against pot side to thicken.
Season, finish with parsley. Serve with crusty bread.

recipes.name: Punjene paprike
recipes.description: Stuffed peppers from Slavonia. Peppers filled with rice, vegetables and cheese, baked in tomato sauce.
instructions: Hollow out peppers, cut tops off and remove seeds.
Cook rice until just tender. Cool slightly.
Mix rice with beaten eggs, cheese, onion, garlic, carrots, paprika, parsley.
Fill peppers generously with rice mixture. Arrange in baking dish.
Mix passata with olive oil and salt. Pour over peppers.
Cover with foil, bake at 190°C for 50 minutes. Uncover, bake 15 min more.
Serve hot with crusty bread and sour cream on the side.

recipes.name: Dalmatinska manistra
recipes.description: Dalmatian pasta with tomato and vegetables. Simple but stunning — pasta is tossed with a rich vegetable sauce.
instructions: Cook pasta in large pot of salted boiling water until al dente.
Sauté onion in olive oil until golden. Add garlic, peppers, zucchini.
Cook vegetables 10 min. Add tomatoes, crush with spoon.
Add wine, chickpeas, chili. Simmer 15 min.
Toss hot drained pasta with sauce, add Parmesan, toss again.
Finish with fresh basil. Serve immediately.

recipes.name: Crna makarona s povrćem
recipes.description: Pasta with black squid-ink color and roasted vegetables. A striking vegetarian dish that captures Dalmatian spirit.
instructions: Cook pasta until al dente. Reserve 2 cups pasta water.
Roast eggplant, zucchini, peppers in oven at 200°C with olive oil for 20 min.
Dissolve squid ink in 100ml warm water. Sauté onion and garlic.
Add tomatoes, simmer. Add roasted vegetables, then ink water.
Toss hot pasta with sauce, add pasta water to loosen. Add Parmesan.
Finish with basil and chili. Serve immediately.

recipes.name: Dalmatinska mana od povrća
recipes.description: Hearty Dalmatian vegetable stew — tomato-based with potatoes, green beans, carrots, and chickpeas.
instructions: Cube potatoes, cut beans, dice carrots and celery.
Sauté onion in olive oil until soft. Add garlic and paprika.
Add tomatoes, break up. Add potatoes and broth.
Simmer 15 min. Add carrots and celery, cook 10 min.
Add beans and chickpeas, cook 15 min more.
Season, scatter parsley. Serve with crusty bread.

recipes.name: Jelaštron
recipes.description: Traditional Continental cabbage rolls — rice and herb filling, steamed in tomato sauce. Can be made vegetarian.
instructions: Blanch cabbage leaves in boiling water until pliable. Separate leaves.
Cook rice until just tender. Cool. Mix with beaten eggs, onion, garlic, carrots, thyme, parsley.
Fill each cabbage leaf with 2 tablespoons rice mixture. Roll and tuck.
Arrange rolls seam-side down in baking dish.
Mix passata with paprika and olive oil, pour over rolls.
Cover tightly, bake at 180°C for 50 minutes. Uncover, bake 15 min.
Serve with sour cream on the side.

recipes.name: Dalmatinski tanjir s domaćim sirom
recipes.description: A stunning Dalmatian appetizer plate: domestic cheese, roasted red peppers, olives, figs and Dalmatian herbs.

ingredients.name: domestic cheese or young Pecorino Sardo

instructions: Slice or break cheese into rough chunks or shave with a cheese plane.
Roast bell peppers: char over flame, peel, slice into strips.
Arrange olives in a small bowl with olive oil and rosemary.
Halve fresh figs and arrange on a large serving platter.
Fan cheese chunks and pepper strips around the board.
Tuck walnut halves and dried figs between the elements.
Drizzle balsamic reduction over the cheese. Scatter sea salt flakes.
Finish with rosemary sprigs. Serve with crusty bread.

recipes.name: Zagorski štrukli
recipes.description: The most famous Zagorje dish — cheese-filled pasta dumplings. Traditionally made with fresh cottage cheese.
instructions: Mix flour, eggs, water and salt into smooth dough. Rest 30 min.
Mix cottage cheese with 3 beaten eggs, chives, salt.
Roll dough thin (3mm). Spread cheese filling evenly.
Roll up dough like a log, cut into 3cm pieces.
Boil in salted water until they float (3-4 min).
Drain. Toss with melted butter, then bread crumbs.
Arrange in baking dish, pour sour cream over top. Bake at 190°C 15 min until golden.

recipes.name: Punjjeni šampinjoni
recipes.description: Large stuffed mushrooms baked under peka-like lid. A family-friendly baked dish the kids love.
instructions: Hollow out mushroom caps, reserve flesh. Chop flesh finely.
Sauté onion and mushroom flesh in butter until soft. Add garlic.
Mix bread crumbs, cheese, beaten eggs, sautéed mixture, oregano, salt, pepper.
Fill mushroom caps generously with filling.
Arrange in baking dish. Bake at 190°C for 25 minutes.
Serve hot as a main or starter.

recipes.name: Dalmatinska mana od gljiva
recipes.description: Dalmatian mushroom stew — hearty mushrooms in tomato-wine sauce, perfect over polenta or crusty bread.
instructions: Clean and slice mushrooms. Sauté in batches in olive oil until golden.
Remove mushrooms. Sauté onion until soft. Add garlic and caraway seeds.
Add paprika, cook 2 min. Add passata and wine, bring to simmer.
Return mushrooms to pan. Add broth, simmer 15 min.
Season, finish with parsley. Serve over creamy polenta.

recipes.name: Crni makaroni
recipes.description: Pasta with squid ink color, tomato and Mediterranean vegetables — dramatic look, vegetarian soul.
instructions: Cook pasta until al dente. Reserve pasta water.
Dissolve squid ink in 100ml warm water.
Roast eggplant and zucchini at 200°C with olive oil for 20 min.
Sauté onion, add garlic and tomatoes, simmer 10 min.
Add ink water, roasted vegetables, wine. Simmer 10 min.
Toss hot pasta with sauce, add pasta water to loosen. Add Parmesan.
Finish with basil and chili. Serve immediately.

recipes.name: Vegetarijanski ćevap
recipes.description: Vegetarian version of ćevap — egg and cheese patties shaped like mini sausages, griddle-fried until golden.
instructions: Mix mashed potatoes, cheese, eggs, flour, onion, garlic, baking soda, salt, pepper, paprika.
Shape into finger-length patties (about 80g each).
Heat griddle or heavy pan. Brush with oil.
Cook patties 3-4 min per side until golden and crisp.
Serve in flatbread with ajvar relish and raw onion rings.
Kids love them — crispy outside, soft inside.

recipes.name: Povrtna pljeskavica
recipes.description: Vegetable patties — grated potatoes and zucchini with egg and cheese, griddled until golden. Kids love them.
instructions: Mix potatoes, zucchini, cheese, eggs, onion, garlic, flour, bread crumbs.
Season with salt, pepper, paprika, oregano. Shape into 9 large patties.
Heat griddle. Brush patties with olive oil.
Griddle 4-5 min per side until deep golden crust forms.
Serve in lepinja buns with ajvar, kajmak, and raw onion rings.
Kids love the crispy crust and soft interior.

recipes.name: Punjene tikvice
recipes.description: Zucchini boats stuffed with rice, eggs and cheese — a summer favourite across Croatia. Kids love scooping out the filling.
instructions: Halve zucchini lengthwise. Scoop out centres, reserve flesh. Chop flesh.
Sauté onion and zucchini flesh until soft. Add garlic.
Cook rice until just tender. Cool. Mix with beaten eggs, cheese, dill.
Stuff zucchini halves generously with rice filling. Place in baking dish.
Mix passata with paprika and olive oil, pour around the boats.
Cover, bake at 190°C for 35 min. Uncover, bake 15 min until golden.
Serve hot with sour cream.

recipes.name: Japrak
recipes.description: Rice-stuffed grape leaves rolled by hand — a Mediterranean Dalmatian classic. Without meat, they are lighter and just as flavourful.
instructions: Blanch grape leaves in boiling water 2 min. Drain, cool.
Cook rice until just tender. Mix with beaten eggs, onion, garlic, dill, thyme.
Place 2 teaspoons filling on each leaf. Roll tightly, tuck ends in.
Arrange rolls seam-side down in baking dish. Brush with olive oil.
Pour passata over rolls. Cover tightly. Bake at 180°C 50 min.
Serve with sour cream. Kids love unwrapping them.

recipes.name: Arambašići
recipes.description: Dalmatian sauerkraut rolls — rice and herb filling rolled in sauerkraut leaves, baked in tomato sauce. Lighter without meat.
instructions: Rinse and squeeze sauerkraut flat. If leaves are too large, cut in half.
Cook rice until just tender. Mix with beaten eggs, onion, garlic, cheese, carrots.
Place filling on each sauerkraut leaf. Roll tightly, tuck ends in.
Arrange rolls seam-side down in baking dish. Brush tops with olive oil.
Mix passata with paprika, pour over rolls. Add bay leaves.
Cover, bake at 180°C for 55 min. Serve with sour cream.

recipes.name: Paški sir pečen
recipes.description: Baked Paški cheese with roasted peppers and olives — a dramatic vegetarian centrepiece for the table.

ingredients.name: Paški cheese or aged Pecorino Sardo
roasted red bell peppers from jar

instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Slice cheese into 2cm slabs.
Place cheese on baking tray. Drizzle with olive oil and honey.
Grate garlic over, scatter rosemary. Season with pepper.
Bake 12-15 minutes until cheese softens and edges blister.
Arrange roasted peppers and olives on serving board.
Place hot cheese in centre. Scatter walnuts and oregano.
Serve immediately with torn crusty bread.

recipes.name: Istarski fuži
recipes.description: Home-made Istrian pasta — fresh egg pasta with butter and Parmesan. Simple, beautiful, timeless Istrian comfort food.
instructions: Mix flour, eggs, olive oil and salt into smooth dough. Rest 30 min.
Roll thin (2mm). Cut into diamond shapes (fuži).
Boil potatoes. Cook pasta in salted water until al dente.
Drain. Toss hot pasta with butter, potatoes, and Parmesan.
Add crumbled cheese and chives. Toss well.
Serve with extra Parmesan and black pepper.

recipes.name: Istarski tartufi
recipes.description: Simple pasta with truffle oil, butter and Parmesan — the luxury of Istria on a plate. Elegant enough for guests.
instructions: Cook pasta until al dente. Reserve 2 cups pasta water.
Meanwhile, make creamy polenta with butter and Parmesan.
Whisk truffle oil and olive oil together.
Drain pasta. Toss with butter first, then truffle oil mixture.
Add Parmesan, toss vigorously. Add pasta water to loosen.
Serve on a pool of polenta. Scatter chives, sea salt, pepper.
Halve hard-boiled eggs and arrange on top.
